info.title: Paycor Public API v2
info.description: Welcome to Paycor's Public API! This document is a reference for the APIs Paycor has available, and acts as a complement to the "Guides" section. # Getting Started To learn more about getting started with Paycor's Public APIs, check out our Guides. # GET, PUT, POST, PATCH * When requesting object, use GET endpoints. All list endpoints support paging, as described [in the other doc]. * When creating an object, use POST endpoints. Your code will need to create an object and send it to Paycor in your API call request body as JSON. You can use the "request sample" body as a starting point. Our endpoints will return a reference to the created object (the ID and GET API URL) for your system. * When updating an object, you will use PUT endpoints. The general flow would be to: GET the object you want to update, modify the fields as desired, then PUT the object (as JSON in the request body) to our endpoints. Some fields like the object's ID cannot be updated because they are system-set. * PATCH - partial update to existing resource # Error Handling * 202 - Response to a successful request that has been received and accepted for processing, but processing has not yet been completed * 400: Please consult the response text to correct your request information. * 401 with response "Access denied due to missing subscription key": Please include your APIM Subscription Key as header Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key or querystring parameter subscription-key. * 401 with no response: Please ensure you included a valid & current Access Token in the Authorization header. * 403: Please ensure your Access Token's scope has all the relevant access you need, on the AppCreator Data Access screen. * 404: Please validate the API route you are using. If that is correct, one of your IDs most likely does not exist or is not in a valid state. * 429: Paycor implements rate limits for our Public API. Each customer (implemented via APIM subscription key) has a limited number of calls. The number of calls is counted across all APIs, not per individual API. Please use bulk endpoints where available and spread your calls over a wider timespan. * The default rate limit is up to 1000 API calls per minute (total across all our Public APIs). * 500: Please contact Paycor. When you make a POST or PUT call and receive a 500, please do not retry the call automatically - this may result in double-posting. GETs can be safely retried. # IDs * ClientId = LegalEntityId * TenantId = CompanyId * EmployeeId is not visible in Paycor's UI, you must retrieve it from the Public API # Earnings, Deductions, Taxes This section describes the domain model for Paycor's Earnings, Deductions, and Taxes. This will provide background for many paydata-related Public API endpoints. Paycor stores Earnings, Deductions, and Taxes each at three levels: * Global: Same data across all legal entities. Setup by Paycor for customers to choose from. Sample Codes (note these will not be setup on every Legal Entity): * Earnings: REG, OT * Taxes: FITWH, SOC, SOCER, OHCIN * Deductions: 401k, KMat, H125, UWay * Legal Entity or Tenant: Codes setup & customized on the legal entity or Tenant level. Must be tied to a Global Code.. * Perform UI allows creating Deduction and Earning codes on Tenant level (under Configure Company nav menu). These will be returned by the Legal Entity Public API endpoints. * Employee: codes setup on a particular employee, tied to a Legal Entity-level or Tenant-level code * Employee Earnings/Deductions/Taxes are applied during payroll. Many properties are inherited from the Legal Entity or Global levels, but some can be overridden. # Authentication

post.summary: Add Employee Hours And Earnings To Paygrid
post.description:
Required parameters in body are: IntegrationVendor, ProcessId, LegalEntityId, EmployeeId, DepartmentCode, EarningCode, BusinessStartDate
            
Data Access: Import Employee Hours and Earnings to Paygrid
post.operationId: Add Employee Hours And Earnings To Paygrid v2

parameters.name: legalEntityId
parameters.description: ID of the Legal Entity for which you want to set employee hours and earnings

parameters.name: replaceData
parameters.description: If "True" is passed for this query parameter and a matching ProcessID is passed, then the system will fully replace the entire record that was previously added.

parameters.name: appendData
parameters.description: If "True" is passed for this query parameter, then the system will NOT replace any record that was previously added.

get.description: Data Access= View Legal Entity Imported Payroll Hours

parameters.name: legalEntityId
parameters.description: ID of the Legal Entity

parameters.name: processId
parameters.description: Optional Process ID to filter results

parameters.name: businessStartDate
parameters.description: Optional business start date to filter results

parameters.name: businessEndDate
parameters.description: Optional business end date to filter results

post.description: Data Access: Import Payroll Hours to Employee
post.operationId: Import Payroll Hours to Employee v2

parameters.name: employeeId
parameters.description: ID of an Employee for whom you want to add the payroll hours

get.description:
Tip: you can retrieve a valid EmployeeID guid via endpoints like 'Get Employees by Legal Entity ID'
            
Data Access: View Employee Information

parameters.name: employeeId
parameters.description: ID of the Employee you want to get

parameters.name: include
parameters.description:
Options to include more data: All, EmploymentDates, Status, Position, WorkLocation
            
Data Access required
            
EmploymentDates = View Employee Employment Dates
            
Status = View Employee Status
            
Position = View Employee Position
            
WorkLocation = View Employee Work Location

parameters.name: emailType
parameters.description: Options to specify which email address to return. Work email type will be returned if none are specified: Home, Work

200.description: Returns an Employee object based on the EmployeeID, with some fields populated based on the Include parameter.

Status = View Legal Entity Employees Status

parameters.name: emailType
parameters.description: Options to specify which email address to return. Work email type will be returned if none are specified: Home, Work

parameters.name: statusFilter
parameters.description: Option to filter by employment status.

parameters.name: employeeNumber
parameters.description: Option to filter by Employee Number.

parameters.name: lastName
parameters.description: Option to filter by Employee Last Name.

parameters.name: departmentCode

parameters.name: workLocationName
parameters.description: Option to filter by Work Location Name.

parameters.name: workerCategory
parameters.description: Option to filter by Worker Category.

parameters.name: workCountryCode
parameters.description:
Option to filter by Work Country Code.
             This field only works when using the "Unpaid" WorkerCategory query param, av= https://www.iban.com/country-codes provides Alpha 2 code for each country that should be used for WorkCountryCode filter. The exceptions are 1) It is USA instead of US for United States and 2) It is UK instead of GB for United Kingdom

parameters.name: continuationToken
parameters.description: Token to get the next set of employees

200.description:
Returns Employee objects based on the Legal Entity ID & Include parameter provided.
            
This endpoint returns all Employee details (based on the Include parameter) except for SSN, DOB, and Emergency Contact. Those fields are not currently available in bulk.
Returns 200 records per page to allow for ease of use for larger clients.

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: ID of the tenant for which you want to get employees

parameters.name: statusFilter
parameters.description: Option to filter by employment status

parameters.name: employeeNumber
parameters.description:
Option to filter by Employee Number.
            This filtering capability is currently non-functional and is expected to be released by mid-January 2025, sorry for the inconvenience at this time.

parameters.name: lastName
parameters.description: Option to filter by Employee Last Name.

parameters.name: departmentCode
parameters.description: Option to filter by Department Code.

parameters.name: workLocationName
parameters.description: Option to filter by Work Location Name.

parameters.name: jobCode
parameters.description: Option to filter by Job Code.

parameters.name: continuationToken
parameters.description: Token to get the next set of employees

200.description: Returns a collection of EmployeeList (with a subset of Employee fields) objects based on the TenantID provided.

post.description:
This immediately "hires" a new employee and associated Person in Paycor's system. There is no way to undo or reverse this operation.
After creating an employee, please wait sixty seconds before using employeeId to call other endpoints.
            
Data Access: Create Employee

get.summary: Get employees in the onboarding state
get.description:
This endpoint allows partners to see the employees in the onboarding state.
            
Data Access: View Legal Entity Onboarding Employees

parameters.name: legalEntityId
parameters.description: ID of the legal entity for which you want to get the employees in the onboarding state

parameters.name: continuationToken
parameters.description: Token to get next set of Onboarding Employee records

200.description: Returns a paged list of Onboarding Employee items.

get.summary: Get Legal Entity Employees Time Card Punches by Employee ID
get.description:
This pulls Punches data from Paycor's Perform Time system for one employee.
* Clients *do not* need to run payroll before hours are returned by this endpoint.
* Our Public API currently does not include whether the hours have already been Approved or not.
* Time Card punches may be delayed by 20 minutes.
            
Date requirements=
* Start Date and End Date must not be more than one year ago
* Start Date must not be in future
* Start date and end date range can be no greater than 90 days
* Only dates will be considered, time will be set to 00=00=00 for start date and 23=59=59 for end date
            
Data Access= View Employee Time Card Punches
            
Endpoint can only be used if the Client has the Perform Time Service
get.operationId: Get Employee Time Card Punches by Employee Id v2

parameters.name: employeeId
parameters.description: ID of employee for which you want to get hours

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Date range filter, showing which records to return

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: Date range filter, showing which records to return

parameters.name: continuationToken
parameters.description: Token to get next set of Employee Time Cards. ContinuationToken would be valid only for 24 hours. If a call is made after 24 hours with old continuationToken, no data will be retrieved.

200.description: Employee Time Card collection, based on Legal Entity Id and provided date ranges

get.summary: Get Legal Entity Employees Time Card Punches by Legal Entity ID
get.description:
This pulls Punches data from Paycor's Perform Time system, for one legal entity.
* Clients *do not* need to run payroll before hours are returned by this endpoint.
* Our Public API currently does not include whether the hours have already been Approved or not.
* Time Card hours may be delayed by 20 minutes.
            
Date requirements=
* Start Date and End Date must not be more than one year ago
* Start Date must not be in future
* Start date and end date range can be no greater than 90 days
* Only dates will be considered, time will be set to 00=00=00 for start date and 23=59=59 for end date

Data Access= View Employee Time Card Punches By Legal Entity Id
            
Endpoint can only be used if the Client has the Perform Time Service
get.operationId: Get Employee Time Card Punches by Legal Entity Id v2

parameters.name: legalEntityId
parameters.description: ID of legal entity for which you want to get hours

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Date range filter, showing which records to return

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: Date range filter, showing which records to return

parameters.name: continuationToken
parameters.description: Token to get next set of Employee Time Cards. ContinuationToken would be valid only for 24 hours. If a call is made after 24 hours with old continuationToken, no data will be retrieved.

200.description: Employee Time Card collection, based on Legal Entity Id and provided date ranges

post.summary: Add Labor Code to Legal Entity
post.description:
This immediately creates a new labor code related to a Legal Entity in Paycor's system. There is no way to undo or reverse this operation.
* Required body attributes:
    * LaborCategoryId
    * Code
    * LaborCodeName
    
To make this call you will need the Job Costing or Workforce Management Service.

Data Access: Create and Update Labor Codes
post.operationId: Add Labor Code to Legal Entity

parameters.name: legalEntityId
parameters.description: ID of the Legal Entity for which you want to create Labor Code

get.summary: Get Legal Entity Departments by Legal Entity ID
get.description: Data Access: View Legal Entity Departments Information
get.operationId: Get Legal Entity Departments by Legal Entity ID

parameters.name: legalEntityId
parameters.description: ID of the Legal Entity for which you want to get the Departments

parameters.name: continuationToken
parameters.description: Token to get the next set of Legal Entity Departments

200.description: Returns Legal Entity Departments based on the Legal Entity ID provided.

put.summary: Update Legal Entity Department by Legal Entity ID
put.description:
Updates existing Department for a Legal Entity.
* The update of the Department will take at least 60 seconds to propagate through the system
* When updating top level departments, payroll id should be used as parent id

Data Access: Create and Update Legal Entity Departments
put.operationId: Update Legal Entity Departments by Department ID

parameters.name: legalEntityId
parameters.description: ID of the Legal Entity for which you want to update the Department

201.description: Returns a resource reference of department

post.summary: Create Departments With Custom Fields by Legal Entity Id
post.description:
Creates new Department With Custom Fields for a Legal Entity.
* the newly created Department With Custom Fields will take at least 60 seconds to propagate through the system

Data Access= Create and Update Legal Entity Departments
post.operationId: Create Departments by Legal Entity Id

parameters.name: legalEntityId
parameters.description: ID of the Legal Entity for which you want to create the Departments With Custom Fields

get.summary: Get Legal Entity Department by Legal Entity ID and Department ID
get.description: Data Access: View Legal Entity Departments by Department Id
get.operationId: Get Legal Entity Departments by Legal Entity ID And ID

parameters.name: legalEntityId
parameters.description: ID of the Legal Entity for which you want to get the Departments

parameters.name: departmentId
parameters.description: ID of the Department

200.description: Returns a Legal Entity Department based on the Legal Entity ID and Department ID provided.

get.summary: Get Labor Codes With Rates by Legal Entity ID
get.description:
Returns a paginated collection of labor codes with associated rates for a Legal Entity.
            
To make this call you will need the Job Costing or Workforce Management Service.
            
Data Access= View Labor Codes by Legal Entity Id
get.operationId: Get Labor Codes With Rates by Legal Entity Id

parameters.name: legalEntityId
parameters.description: ID of the Legal Entity for which you want to retrieve Labor Codes With Rates

parameters.name: continuationToken
parameters.description: Token to get the next set of results

200.description: Returns a paged collection of labor codes with rates.

post.description:
This operation creates a labor code with associated rates for a Legal Entity in Paycor's system.
* Required body attributes=
    * LaborCategoryId
    * LaborCodeName
    * LaborCode
    * Rates[].Action (permitted values= A, D)
    * Rates[].EffectiveStartDate
    * Rates[].Rate (required when Action is 'A')
            
To make this call you will need the Job Costing or Workforce Management Service.
            
Data Access= Create and Update Labor Codes

200.description: Labor code and all rates created successfully.

207.description: Labor code was created but some rates failed. Response body contains error details.

400.description: Invalid request body. Response body contains validation error details.

put.summary: Update Work Location By Legal Entity ID
put.description: Data Access: Update Legal Entity Work Location

parameters.name: legalEntityId
parameters.description: ID of the Legal Entity for which you want to get the work locations

PaycorError.description: The Paycor Error model is used to catch exceptions and related contextual data for logging.

correlationId.description: Correlation ID will help identify specific transactions and logs when troubleshooting.  This is generated by Paycor systems.

title.description: Title is friendly overall information that can be displayed to users.

detail.description: Gives more information to describe what the error is.
detail.example: A validation error occurred. See the source for more information about specific errors.

source.description: Specific details of the fields that have errors (when applicable).

PaycorRateLimitError.description: The Paycor Error model is used to catch exceptions and related contextual data for logging.

message.description: Message is friendly overall information that can be displayed to users.
message.example: Rate limit is exceeded. Try again in 60 seconds.

ImportPayrollHours.description: Request body object for importing Payroll data into Paycor's Payroll system (not Time system).

integrationVendor.description: Required freeform field for tracking purposes. You can input your company or application name.

processId.description:
Unique identifier of the transaction to prevent double-posting in Paycor's systems. 
Please generate a new GUID (using any method) for every API call.
Use the same GUID only when you want to replace existing data.

importEmployees.description: List of Employees, with nested lists of Earnings, to import into Paycor's Paygrid system.

employeeId.description: Unique identifier of the employee in Paycor's system. Generated by Paycor.

importEarnings.description: List of Earnings for this Employee.

baseDepartmentCode.description: Department to be used when employee is being paid outside of their assigned payroll.  All scheduled earnings for this employee will be paid in this department.

workLocationId.description: Unique identifier of the work location in Paycor's system. Generated by Paycor. Optional parameter.

ImportEarning.description: Employee-specific Payroll Import row for a specific working date range. Includes all Pay-related info.

departmentCode.description: Department code that the Hours should be paid under. You can use GET Legal Entity Departments to retrieve valid Code values.

earningCode.description: Employee's earning Code. You can retrieve via Get Employee Earnings endpoints.

earningHours.description: Number of hours that Employee worked.

earningRate.description: Dollar rate to assign to the earning.

dateWorked.description:
The date worked (associated with this earning).
Optional. May be used by clients who have Workweek calculation feature enabled or by specific partner integrations.

laborCodes.description: Optional. This allows supplying Labor Codes tied to this earning, used for Job Costing and other functionality.

payGroupId.description: Unique identifier of the pay group in Paycor's system. You can retrieve by calling 'Get Pay Groups by Legal Entity ID'

payNumber.description:
Optional, defaults to 1.
Indicates which paycheck for this employee the earning will appear on (essentially a sort order for the particular Employee).
Use sequential values such as 1, 2, 3...

clientDefinedValue.description: Optional. This field allows passing in additional information used by specific partner integrations.

LaborCode.description:
Labor Code (also called Job Code) is used for Job Costing functionality.
Note that both Category and Code are required in order to be unique.

category.description:
Job Category (also known as Labor Category) are used to organize Job Codes.
This feature is used for Job Costing support, which not all clients will use. Categories must be configured in advance by an HR Admin in Perform.
You can use GET Legal Entity Job Categories API to retrieve.

code.description:
Job Code is used for Job Costing functionality, to map paid amounts to a project.
You can use GET Legal Entity Job Codes APIs to retrieve. This field expects the Code, not the Labor Code Name.
Not all clients will use Job Costing.

allOf.description: Employee's timecard base object used for Payroll Api

employeeId.description: Unique identifier of the employee in the tenant. You can use any GET Employee endpoint to retrieve.

timeCardData.description: List of the employee's earning rates, factors and amounts of type EmployeeEarningAmount.

workLocationId.description: Unique identifier of the work location in Paycor's system. Generated by Paycor. Optional parameter.

TimeCardData.description: Employee's timecard with information about earnings, start and end dates.

earningCode.description: Employee's earning Code. You can retrieve via Get Employee Earnings endpoints.

EmployeeTimeCardBase.description: Employee's timecard base object used for Payroll Api

appId.description: Optional field that can be used for tracking purposes. Not required for payroll processing.

integrationVendor.description: Required freeform field for tracking purposes. You can input your company or application name.

processId.description:
Unique identifier of the transaction to prevent double-posting in Paycor's systems. 
Please generate a new GUID (using any method) for every API call.

legalEntityId.description: Unique identifier of the Legal Entity in Paycor's system.

departmentCode.description: Department code that the Hours should be paid under. You can use GET Legal Entity Departments to retrieve valid Code values.

jobCode.description: JobCode parameter is not currently used - included for future functionality.

Email.description: The Email model represents Email information.

type.description: Enumeration of valid email type values.

EmailType.description: Enumeration of valid Email Type values.

allOf.description:
The Employee model represents all Employee related information.
 Provides links to the Employee's associated Person, Legal Entity, Department, Earning, Deduction, Tax, Direct Deposit, Pay Rate and Custom Field information.

positionData.description: Data including the employee's position of type EmployeePositionData.

employeeNumber.description: Unique number of the employee in the tenant. Generated by Paycor.

workCountryCode.description:
Provides Alpha 2 code for each country that should be used for WorkCountryCode filter.
The exceptions are 1) It is USA instead of US for United States and 2) It is UK instead of GB for United Kingdom.

lastName2.description: Additional last name of the employee.

workerCategory.description:
Determines if an employee is paid via Paycor’s payroll system or unpaid.
Options:
Paid: Default value if left empty or invalid.
Unpaid: Only if the client has the “Unpaid Worker” service enabled. Defaults to “Paid” if the service is not enabled.
Choose “Unpaid” for third-party paid or volunteer positions with no salary information.

EmployeePositionData.description: The Employee Position Data model represents the Employee's Position related data.

jobTitle.description: Name of the employee's job title.

effectiveEndDate.description:
Date the employee position data is no longer in effect. This property will not return values for Active employees
Format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ  (ISO-8601 standard)

departmentEffectiveDate.description:
Date the employee's current department assignment took effect. 
Format= YYYY-MM-DDTHH=MM=SSZ (ISO-8601 standard, UTC).

payGroupId.description: Unique identifier of the pay group in Paycor's system. Generated by Paycor.

manager.description: Employee's Manager's data of type Manager.

Manager.description:
The Manager model represents information about the Employee's Manager.
Provides link to the Manager's Employee level information.

id.description: Unique identifier of the manager in Paycor's system. Generated by Paycor.

employeeNumber.description: Employee number of the employee's manager.

employee.description: URL to access the Employee resource for additional information about the employee's manager.

ResourceReference.description: The Resource Reference model is used to provide URL links to resources.

id.description: ID of the resource being referenced. This is generated by Paycor.

url.description: URL to reach the resource being referenced

EmployeeBase.description:
The Employee model represents all Employee related information.
 Provides links to the Employee's associated Person, Legal Entity, Department, Earning, Deduction, Tax, Direct Deposit, Pay Rate and Custom Field information.

id.description: Unique identifier of the employee in Paycor's system. Generated by Paycor.

employmentDateData.description: Data including the employee's employment dates of type EmploymentDateData.

statusData.description: Data including the employee's status of type EmployeeStatusData.

workLocation.description: Data including the employee's work location of type EmployeeWorkLocationData.

earnings.description: URL to access the Earnings resource for employee earnings information.

deductions.description: URL to access the Deductions resource for employee deductions information.

taxes.description: URL to access the Taxes resource for employee tax information.

directDeposits.description: URL to access the Direct Deposits resource for employee direct deposit information.

payRates.description: URL to access the PayRates resource for employee pay rate information.

legalEntity.description: URL to access the Legal Entity resource.

person.description: URL to access the Persons resource for employee information.

department.description: URL to access the Department resource for information about the employee's department.

EmploymentDateData.description: The Employment Date Data represents the Employee's Employment Date information.

hireDate.description:
Date the employee was hired.
Format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ  (ISO-8601 standard)

adjustedHireDate.description:
Adjusted hire date is used when the employee's hire date does not accurately reflect benefits eligibility because an employee may have left,
been rehired or taken a Leave of Absence. This provides an alternative date for the employee's accruals to be calculated on.
 Format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ  (ISO-8601 standard)

terminationDate.description:
Date the employee was terminated.
Format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ  (ISO-8601 standard)

reHireDate.description:
Re-hire date of the employee. Terminated employees can be rehired.
 Format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ  (ISO-8601 standard)

seniorityDate.description:
Date on which the calculation of an employee's seniority is based.
Format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ  (ISO-8601 standard)

EmployeeStatusData.description: The Employee Status Data model represents Employee Employment Status information.

status.description: Enumeration of valid employment status values.

flsa.description: Enumeration of valid Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) defined type values.

type.description: Enumeration of valid employment type values.

eligibleForRehire.description: Determines is employee eligible for rehire.

leaveStartDate.description: The date on which the employee's leave begins.

leaveReason.description: The reason for the employee's leave.

EmploymentStatus.description: Enumeration of valid Employment Status values.

FlsaType.description: Enumeration of valid Types of Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) employment values.

EmploymentType.description: Enumeration of valid Employment Type values.

EmployeeWorkLocationData.description: The Employee Work Location Data model represents the Employee's Work Location related data.

id.description: Unique identifier of the Employee's work location in Paycor's system. Generated by Paycor.

name.description: Name of the Employee's work location.
name.example: ACME East is an open concept office.

streetLine1.description: First line of street address information.

type.description: Address Type of Employee Work Location.

WorkLocationAddressType.description: Enumeration of valid Type of Work Location Address values.

Includes.description:
All - Include all data


    EmploymentDates - Include employment dates (e.g. hireDate)


    Position - Include employee position (e.g. job title and manager)


    Status - Include status data (e.g. Full time)


    WorkLocation - Include work location data

PagedResultOfEmployeeReturnItem.description:
The Paged Result model enables pagination of results if maximum of 100 records per response is exceeded. Additional
results can be requested by providing calling the AdditionalResultsUrl endpoint containing the continuation token, or
by building your own URL using the provided continuation token.

hasMoreResults.description: Whether additional API result records exist on the Paycor system.

continuationToken.description:
Paycor generated token string which may be provided in a subsequent request to  release additional records past the maximum. 
Used when partner is building their own URL.

additionalResultsUrl.description: Provides URL to call with the continuation token included for additional results.

records.description: List of the API response records.

Includes2.description:
All - Include all data


    EmploymentDates - Include employment dates (e.g. hireDate)


    Position - Include employee position (e.g. job title and manager)


    Status - Include status data (e.g. Full time)


    WorkLocation - Include work location data

PagedResultOfEmployeeList.description:
The Paged Result model enables pagination of results if maximum of 100 records per response is exceeded. Additional
results can be requested by providing calling the AdditionalResultsUrl endpoint containing the continuation token, or
by building your own URL using the provided continuation token.

hasMoreResults.description: Whether additional API result records exist on the Paycor system.

continuationToken.description:
Paycor generated token string which may be provided in a subsequent request to  release additional records past the maximum. 
Used when partner is building their own URL.

additionalResultsUrl.description: Provides URL to call with the continuation token included for additional results.

records.description: List of the API response records.

EmployeeList.description:
The Employee List model represents information about multiple Employees.
Provides link to additional Employee information.

id.description: Unique identifier of the employee list record in Paycor's system. Generated by Paycor.

employeeNumber.description: Unique number of the employee in a partner's tenant.

employee.description: URL to access the Employee resource for additional information about the employee.

PagedResultOfEmployeesIdentifyingData.description:
The Paged Result model enables pagination of results if maximum of 100 records per response is exceeded. Additional
results can be requested by providing calling the AdditionalResultsUrl endpoint containing the continuation token, or
by building your own URL using the provided continuation token.

hasMoreResults.description: Whether additional API result records exist on the Paycor system.

continuationToken.description:
Paycor generated token string which may be provided in a subsequent request to  release additional records past the maximum. 
Used when partner is building their own URL.

additionalResultsUrl.description: Provides URL to call with the continuation token included for additional results.

records.description: List of the API response records.

socialSecurityNumber.description: Social security number of the employee.

employeeId.description: Unique identifier of the employee in Paycor's system. Generated by Paycor.

employeeNumber.description: Unique number of the employee in the client.

birthDate.description:
Date of birth of the employee.
Format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ  (ISO-8601 standard)

status.description: Employee status. Values are: Active and Inactive

EmploymentStatusIdentifyingData.description: Enumeration of valid Employment Status values.

resourceUrl.description: URL to access the resource information about the entity that was created or updated.

allOf.description: The Create Employee model represents Employee related information needed to create a new employee (a new hire).

employeeNumber.description: Unique number of the employee in the tenant. Generated by Paycor if not provided.

isFullTime.description: Define if employee is full time or not.

Employee3.description: The Create Employee model represents Employee related information needed to create a new employee (a new hire).

prefix.description: Enumeration of valid name prefix values.

suffix.description: Enumeration of valid name suffix values.

homeEmail.description: Home Email Information of an employee.

workEmail.description: Work Email Information of an employee.

phones.description:
List of type Phone containing phone numbers of the employee.
Accepts home, mobile and work phone numbers, upto 1 of each type.
Mobile phone is accepted only if home contact is provided.

socialSecurityNumber.description: Social security number of the employee.

birthDate.description:
Date of birth of the employee following the ISO 8601 standard.
Format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ  (ISO-8601 standard)

workLocation.description:
The name of the Work Location to associate with new hire.
 * Must be an existing Work Location. Use API 'GET Legal Entity Work Location by Legal Entity ID' to retrieve a list of valid names.

jobTitle.description:
Name of the Job Title to associate with new hire.
 * Must be an existing Job setup on the Tenant. Use API 'GET Tenant Job Titles by TenantId' to retrieve a list of valid names.

hireDate.description:
Date the employee was hired following the ISO 8601 standard.
Format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ  (ISO-8601 standard)

reHireDate.description:
Re-hire date of the employee. Terminated employees can be rehired.
 Format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ  (ISO-8601 standard)

flsa.description: Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) type value for employee (enumeration)

managerEmpId.description: Unique identifier of the manager in Paycor's system. Generated by Paycor.

paygroupDescription.description:
The description of the paygroup that the employee belongs to.
 * Must be existing Paygroup. Call GET Pay Groups by LegalEntityID to lookup valid values in the field "PaygroupName".

departmentCode.description:
The department code that the employee belongs to.
  * Must be existing Department. Call Get Legal Entity Departments by LegalEntityID to get valid Code value.

veteran.description: Veteran Status of new hire employee (enumeration)

disability.description: Disabiilty Status of new hire employee (enumeration)

Phone.description: The Phone model represents Phone information.

countryCode.description: Country code for the phone number.

areaCode.description: Area code for the phone number. This property is required if data is passed to the "PhoneNumber" property.

phoneNumber.description: Local phone number. This property is required if data is passed to the "AreaCode" property.

type.description: Enumeration of valid phone type values.

subscriberNumber.description: International Phone Number is a raw local number that can go beyond 10 digits

PhoneType.description: Enumeration of valid Phone Type values.

EthnicityType.description: Enumeration of valid Ethnicity Type values.

MaritalStatus.description: Enumeration of valid Marital Status values.

VeteranStatus.description: Enumeration of valid Veteran Status values.

DisabilityStatus.description: Enumeration of valid Disability Status values.

GenericAddress.description: The Generic Address model represents the Person's Address information (No type).

streetLine1.description: First line of street address information.

streetLine2.description: Additional line of street address information.

PagedResultOfOnboardingEmployeeV2.description:
The Paged Result model enables pagination of results if maximum of 100 records per response is exceeded. Additional
results can be requested by providing calling the AdditionalResultsUrl endpoint containing the continuation token, or
by building your own URL using the provided continuation token.

hasMoreResults.description: Whether additional API result records exist on the Paycor system.

continuationToken.description:
Paycor generated token string which may be provided in a subsequent request to  release additional records past the maximum. 
Used when partner is building their own URL.

additionalResultsUrl.description: Provides URL to call with the continuation token included for additional results.

records.description: List of the API response records.

onboardingEmployeeId.description: Id of the employee in the onboarding state.

firstName.description: First name of the employee in the onboarding state.

lastName.description: Last name of the employee in the onboarding state.

fullName.description: First and last name of the employee in the onboarding state combined.

emailAddress.description: Email address of the employee in the onboarding state.

hasOnboardingAccess.description: Indicates if this is invited or manual hiring.

hireDate.description: Date when the employee was hired.

stepsTotal.description: Number of the total steps in the onboarding.

stepsCompleted.description: Number of the completed steps in the onboarding.

lastEmployeeStepCompleted.description: Last step that was completed by the employee.

lastEmployeeStepCompletedDate.description: Date when the last step was completed by employee.

lastAdminStepCompleted.description: Last step that was completed by the admin.

lastAdminStepCompletedDate.description: Date when the last step was completed by admin.

manager.description: Full name of the employees manager.

The Paged Result model enables pagination of results if maximum of 100 records per response is exceeded. Additional
results can be requested by providing calling the AdditionalResultsUrl endpoint containing the continuation token, or
by building your own URL using the provided continuation token.

hasMoreResults.description: Whether additional API result records exist on the Paycor system.

Paycor generated token string which may be provided in a subsequent request to  release additional records past the maximum. 
Used when partner is building their own URL.

additionalResultsUrl.description: Provides URL to call with the continuation token included for additional results.

records.description: List of the API response records.

employeeNumber.description: Unique number of the Employee in the Legal Entity.

TimeCardBase.description: The Timecard model represents Employee level time, punch details,pay rate (GROSSPAY) and associated labor code information.

displayDate.description:
The date when the timecard was created.
Format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS

punchIn.description:
Date and time when the employee punched in.
Format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS

punchOut.description:
Date and time when the employee punched out.
Format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS

hourAmount.description: Amount of hours entered for employee's timecard.

departmentCode.description: Department Code where the Employee worked.

departmentName.description: Department name where the Employee worked.

earningCode.description: Identifies the type of earning for this timecard.

estimatedGrossPay.description: Employee's estimated pay for the entire Punch pair..

laborCodes.description: List of type LaborCode containing the employee's labor code information.

effectOnEmpEarnings.description: Identifies the Effect on Employee Earnings.

LaborCodeV2.description: The LaborCode model is used to define rules for Employees including minimum and maximum hours, overtime and benefits.

laborCategoryLevel.description:
This represents the Labor Category level the Labor Code is associated with.  
You can find the correlating Labor Category Level by calling GET Labor Categories 
by Legal Entity ID and matching on the "CategoryLevel" field (available values = 1-6).

laborCode.description: The labor code associated with the punch pair.

laborCodeName.description: The labor code name associated with the punch pair.

The Paged Result model enables pagination of results if maximum of 100 records per response is exceeded. Additional
results can be requested by providing calling the AdditionalResultsUrl endpoint containing the continuation token, or
by building your own URL using the provided continuation token.

hasMoreResults.description: Whether additional API result records exist on the Paycor system.

continuationToken.description:
Paycor generated token string which may be provided in a subsequent request to  release additional records past the maximum. 
Used when partner is building their own URL.

additionalResultsUrl.description: Provides URL to call with the continuation token included for additional results.

records.description: List of the API response records.

EmployeeBalance.description: The Employee Balance model represents Employee level Balance information for accrual balances.

employeeNumber.description: Customer-assigned friendly unique number of the employee within the Tenant.

employeeId.description: Unique identifier of the Employee in Paycor's system. Generated by Paycor. Used for other GET endpoints.

typeBalances.description: Employee accrual balances information, for each plan the Employee participates in.

timeOffPlanName.description: Name of the time off plan related to this balance.

timeOffTypeId.description: Unique identifier of the time off plan record in Paycor's system. Generated by Paycor.

timeOffTypeCode.description: Abbreviated code of the time off type linked to this plan (admin-configured)

timeOffTypeName.description: Name of the time off type linked to this plan (admin-configured)

activityStartDate.description: Date range this time off balance record covers.

activityEndDate.description: Date range this time off balance record covers.

currentBalance.description: Current balance (in hours) within this plan and date range, including upcoming approved requests.

accruedBalance.description: Total number of hours accrued (earned) within this plan and date range.

usedBalance.description: Number of hours used (spent) so far within this plan and date range.

scheduledBalance.description: Scheduled upcoming time off within this plan and date range, in hours.

defaultEarning.description: Earning information related to this time off plan.

id.description: Unique identifier of the earning record in Paycor's system. Generated by Paycor.

code.description: Descriptive earning identifier which appears on paychecks. Only set at the legal entity or tenant level.

description.description: Description of the employee earning code.

effectiveStartDate.description:
Date the employee earning take effect.
Format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ  (ISO-8601 standard)

effectiveEndDate.description:
Date the employee earning is no longer in effect.
Format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ  (ISO-8601 standard)

laborCodeName.description: Unique name of labor code in the labor category

description.example: labor code effective until New year

effectiveStartDate.description: Effective start date of labor code

effectiveEndDate.description: Effective end date of labor code

PagedResultOfDepartment.description:
The Paged Result model enables pagination of results if maximum of 100 records per response is exceeded. Additional
results can be requested by providing calling the AdditionalResultsUrl endpoint containing the continuation token, or
by building your own URL using the provided continuation token.

hasMoreResults.description: Whether additional API result records exist on the Paycor system.

continuationToken.description:
Paycor generated token string which may be provided in a subsequent request to  release additional records past the maximum. 
Used when partner is building their own URL.

additionalResultsUrl.description: Provides URL to call with the continuation token included for additional results.

records.description: List of the API response records.

Department.description: The Client Department model represents client department information.

id.description: Unique identifier of the department in Paycor's system. Generated by Paycor.

description.description: User defined description of the department.

parentId.description:
Unique identifier of the department's parent department in Paycor's system.
Value will be NULL for top level departments.
Level 2 Departments do not have ParentID's.

level.description:
The level of the department in the department structure.
Level 1 Department is the parent payroll for the pay group. Hence, Level will always be >= 2 for bulk/list api.

payrollId.description: Id of the parent payroll for the pay group.

payrollDescription.description: Description of the parent payroll for the pay group.

workLocationId.description: Unique identifier of the legal entity's work location in Paycor's system. Generated by Paycor.

workLocationName.description: Name of the legal entity's work location.
workLocationName.example: ACME East is an open concept office.

customFieldValues.description: Specific details of the custom fields vaues assigned to the department.

Department2.description: The Department model represents legal entity department information.

departmentId.description: Unique Identifier of the Department in Paycor's system.

code.description: User defined department code. Only numeric characters are allowed and the limit is 14 characters.

parentId.description:
Id of the parent department (or payroll) under which new department will be created.
When updating top level departments, payroll id should be used as parent id

description.description: User defined description of the department.

workLocationId.description:
The ID of the Work Location to associate with new department.
 * Must be an existing Work Location on the Legal Entity. Use API 'Get a list of Work Locations for a Legal Entity' to retrieve a list of valid IDs.

payrollCustomValues.description: A list of the department's custom field values.

PayrollCustomValues.description: The Payroll Custom Values model represents information about custom field values.

DepartmentsWithCustomFields.description: The DepartmentsWithCustomFields model represents legal entity departmentwithcustomfields information.

code.description:
User defined department code. Only numeric characters are allowed and the limit
 is 14 characters.

parentId.description:
Id of the parent department (or payroll) under which new department will be created.
When creating top level departments, payroll id should be used as parent id

description.description: User defined description of the department.

workLocationId.description:
The ID of the Work Location to associate with new department. * Must be an existing
Work Location on the Legal Entity. Use API 'Get a list of Work Locations for
a Legal Entity' to retrieve a list of valid IDs.

PagedResultOfLaborCodeWithRates.description:
The Paged Result model enables pagination of results if maximum of 100 records per response is exceeded. Additional
results can be requested by providing calling the AdditionalResultsUrl endpoint containing the continuation token, or
by building your own URL using the provided continuation token.

hasMoreResults.description: Whether additional API result records exist on the Paycor system.

continuationToken.description:
Paycor generated token string which may be provided in a subsequent request to  release additional records past the maximum. 
Used when partner is building their own URL.

additionalResultsUrl.description: Provides URL to call with the continuation token included for additional results.

records.description: List of the API response records.

LaborCodeWithRates.description: Represents a labor code and its associated pay rates.

id.description: Unique identifier for the labor code.

laborCategoryId.description: Unique identifier for the labor category.

laborCodeName.description: Display name of the labor code.

laborCode.description: Short code identifying the labor code.

laborCodeDescription.example: Overtime hours worked beyond standard schedule

effectiveStartDate.description: Date when the labor code becomes effective.

effectiveEndDate.description: Date when the labor code expires.

rates.description: List of rates associated with this labor code.

LaborCodeRateWithDepartments.description: Represents a pay rate for a labor code along with the departments it applies to.

jobCodeRateId.description: Unique identifier for the job code rate.

rate.description: Pay rate value for the labor code.

effectiveStartDate.description: Date when the rate becomes effective.

departmentList.description: List of departments associated with this rate.

LaborCodeRateDepartments.description: Represents a department associated with a labor code rate.

LaborCodeWithRatesImportErrorResponse.description:
Returned by the import endpoint when the domain responds HTTP 207 (Multi-Status).
The labor code item itself was saved, but one or more of its submitted rates were rejected.
Source is null for 207 responses; per-rate failures are listed in FailedRates.

correlationId.description: Unique identifier used to correlate this response with server-side logs.

status.description: HTTP status code as a string (e.g. "207").

title.description: Short human-readable title describing the partial failure (e.g. "Partial import failure").

detail.description: Detailed message explaining the overall failure reason.
detail.example: 3 rate(s) failed inside saved labor code. Please read 'failedRates' for details.

source.description: Always null for 207 responses. Rate-level failures are reported in FailedRates instead.

failedRates.description: Individual rates that failed inside an otherwise-saved labor code. Null when there are no rate failures.

example.Reason: Rate with EffectiveStartDate '1900-01-01' could not be found for LaborCode 'LCName'. Verify the date matches an existing rate.

LaborCodeRateFailure.description:
Describes one or more rates rejected for the same reason, linked to their parent labor code item.
Multiple rates that share the same Reason and HasOverlaps value
are collapsed into a single entry; their 1-based positions within the submitted batch are
listed in RowNumbers.

laborCode.description: The LaborCode of the parent item these rates belong to.

rowNumbers.description: 1-based row numbers within the rate batch submitted for this labor code.

hasOverlaps.description: True when the rates were rejected due to overlapping date ranges.

reason.description: Human-readable reason shared by all rows in this entry.
reason.example: Rate date ranges overlap with an existing rate for this labor code.

LaborCodeWithRatesImportRequest.description:
Represents a single labor code upsert request, including its associated rates.
Pass Id as Empty (or omit it) to create a new labor code item;
supply an existing Id to update it.
Each item may carry zero or more LaborCodeRateImportItem entries in Rates.

id.description:
Id of an existing labor category item (JobCategoryItemId).
Omit or pass Guid.Empty to create a new item; supply a value to update an existing one.

laborCategoryId.description:
The Guid of the labor category this item belongs to (JobCategoryId).
Must match an existing labor category for the given ClientId.

laborCodeName.description: Display name of the labor code. Must be unique within the labor category.

laborCode.description: Short labor code identifier (JobIdentifier). Must be unique within the labor category.

laborCodeDescription.description: Optional description of the labor code.

effectiveStartDate.description:
Date from which the labor code is effective.
Defaults to today when omitted.

effectiveEndDate.description:
Date on which the labor code expires. Null means no expiry.
Must be greater than or equal to EffectiveStartDate when provided.

rates.description:
Zero or more rate records to associate with this labor code item.
ClientId, JobCategoryId, and JobCategoryItemId are derived from the parent
request and this item — do not supply them on the rate.

LaborCodeRateImportItem.description:
A single rate record for a labor code item.
ClientId, JobCategoryId, JobCategoryItemId, and UserKey are all derived server-side.
JobCodeRateId is generated server-side on insert; supply EffectiveStartDate to identify a rate for deletion.

action.description:
Operation to perform= "A" = Add (insert new rate), "D" = Delete (soft-delete by EffectiveStartDate).
Defaults to "A". Must match regex [adAD].

rate.description:
The hourly / unit rate.
Must be between 0 and 999999 when provided.

effectiveStartDate.description: Date from which this rate is effective. Required.

effectiveEndDate.description:
Date on which this rate expires. Null means no expiry.
When provided, must be greater than or equal to EffectiveStartDate.

departmentCodeList.description:
Numeric department codes this rate applies to (e.g. ["100", "200"]).
When empty, the rate applies to all departments.
Each value must be numeric and resolvable to a known DepartmentUId for the given ClientId.

WorkLocation.description: The Legal Entity Work Location model represents information about a legal entity’s work location

id.description: Unique identifier of the work location in Paycor's system. Generated by Paycor.

addresses.description: A list of work location's addresses.

phoneNumbers.description: A list of the work location's phone numbers.

payrollCustomValues.description: A list of the work location's custom fields values.

WorkLocationAddressUpdate.description: The Legal Entity Work Location Address update model represents information about a legal entity’s work location address.

type.description: Enumeration of valid address type values.

streetLine1.description: First line of street address information.

streetLine2.description: Additional line of street address information.

AddressType.description: Enumeration of valid Types of Address.

type.description: Enumeration of valid work location phone type values.

PhoneNumberType.description: Enumeration of valid Types of phone number.

PagedResultOfLegalEntityWorkLocationWith.description:
The Paged Result model enables pagination of results if maximum of 100 records per response is exceeded. Additional
results can be requested by providing calling the AdditionalResultsUrl endpoint containing the continuation token, or
by building your own URL using the provided continuation token.

Paycor generated token string which may be provided in a subsequent request to  release additional records past the maximum. 
Used when partner is building their own URL.

LegalEntityWorkLocationWithCustomFields.description:
Represents a legal entity's work location in Paycor's system, including its core details,
addresses, phone numbers, custom field values.

This model is typically returned by the API call 
Get a list of Work Locations for a Legal Entity.

id.description: Unique identifier of the legal entity's work location in Paycor's system. Generated by Paycor.

name.description: Name of the legal entity's work location.
name.example: ACME East is an open concept office.

addresses.description: A list of legal entity's work location's addresses.

phones.description: A list of the legal entity's work location's phone numbers.

locationNumber.description: Location Number of the work location.

storeId.description: Unique identifier of the work location store. StoreId is used for clients with WOTC Service only, defaults to Worklocation name if not provided.

isFmlaEligible.description: Flag which determines if work location is FMLA eligible (Family and Medical Leave Act).

payrollCustomValues.description:
Custom payroll values associated with the work location on the legal entity.
Use API 'Get a list of Work Locations for a Legal Entity' to retrieve.

LegalEntityWorkLocationAddress.description: The Legal Entity Work Location Address model represents Legal Entity Work Location Address information.

streetLine1.description: First line of street address information.

streetLine2.description: Additional line of street address information.

type.description: Enumeration of valid legal entity work location address type values.
Enumeration of valid work location phone type values.

Access-Token.description: You will need to Activate your Application and retrieve an Access Token (a JWT Bearer Token) before calling our APIs. Please login and refer to the Guides for Activation information. When using the 'Try API' feature online, type into the textbox: Bearer {your Access Token}.
Access-Token.name: Authorization

Apim-Subscription-Key.description: Provide the APIm subscription key that you wish to use. Please login and consult the Guides for more information on retrieving this.
Apim-Subscription-Key.name: Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key

tags.name: Employee Payroll Hours
tags.description:
These APIs interact with Paycor's Payroll system directly. They do not go through any Time system.

* Paycor will need to add the 'Integration Payroll Time Partner' or have the Trackforce product enabled.
* They are useful if you are integrating a third-party time product with Paycor.
* You will need to choose an Earning Code for each set of Hours. The Code must already be setup on the particular Employee - you can use the Employee Earnings endpoints for this.
* Calling this endpoint kicks off a backend import process. Currently, there are no APIs to read or monitor the import process. A client administrator must import, review & submit the payrun before any employees will be paid.
* Client administrator will need to login to Paycor's Paygrid and use the Import Time Card feature to see & approve the values. They will see an Import Pay screen when they Begin a new Payrun.

tags.name: Employees
tags.description:
These endpoints interact with Employees.
* An Employee is linked to a single Legal Entity, a single Person, and a single Tenant.
* Each Person will be linked to one or more Employees (setup by the admin in the Manage People screen).
* Employee APIs will return Work email addresses, not Home.

tags.name: Employee Onboarding
tags.description:
These APIs allow working with employees in the onboarding state.

These endpoints can only be used if the Client has the Onboarding Service.

tags.name: Employee Time Card Punches
tags.description:
These APIs interact with Paycor's Perform Time system.
* They will only work with Perform Time-enabled clients - otherwise you will receive an error.
* These APIs will not work for Time on Demand or Attendance on Demand clients.

tags.name: Employee Time Off Accruals
tags.description:
Accruals are time-off benefits for employees that increase over time, and are updated when a payrun is run (the employee is paid).
* Common plans include PTO, vacation time, sick time, and floating holidays.
* Accrual types and plans are setup at the Tenant level in Paycor's UI by the HR Admin.
* Each employee can participate in a number of individual Accrual plans.
* This API is only available to legal entities with the 'Perform Accruals' service. It is not available to clients with the older 'Perform Time Off' service. There is a migration path available.

tags.name: Labor Codes
tags.description: This API allows working with Labor Codes

tags.name: Legal Entity Departments
tags.description: These APIs allow working with the Departments (also called Organizations) that have been configured on the Legal Entity.

tags.name: Labor Codes With Rates
tags.description: This API allows working with Labor Codes With Rates

tags.name: Legal Entity Work Locations
tags.description: These APIs allow working with the Work Locations (also called Organizations) that have been configured on the Legal Entity.
